CREATE TABLE test_char (id INT, char_col CHARACTER(1));
INSERT INTO test_char (id, char_col) VALUES (1, 'A');
INSERT INTO test_char (id, char_col) VALUES (2, 'B');
INSERT INTO test_char (id, char_col) VALUES (3, 'C');
SELECT * FROM test_char WHERE char_col = 'A';
SELECT id FROM test_char WHERE char_col <> 'B';
UPDATE test_char SET char_col = 'D' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, char_col FROM test_char WHERE char_col IN (SELECT char_col FROM test_char WHERE id > 1);
CREATE TABLE test_char_join (id INT, char_col CHAR(1));
INSERT INTO test_char_join (id, char_col) VALUES (1, 'X'), (2, 'Y');
SELECT a.id, a.char_col, b.char_col FROM test_char a JOIN test_char_join b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT * FROM test_char WHERE char_col IN ('E', 'A') OR char_col = 'C';
SELECT char_col FROM test_char WHERE char_col LIKE 'A';
SELECT id FROM test_char WHERE char_col IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO test_char (id, char_col) VALUES (4, ' ');
SELECT * FROM test_char WHERE LENGTH(char_col) <= 1;
CREATE TABLE edge_cases (id INT, char_col CHARACTER(1));
INSERT INTO edge_cases (id, char_col) VALUES (1, 'A');
INSERT INTO edge_cases (id, char_col) VALUES (2, ' ');
SELECT * FROM edge_cases WHERE char_col IN (SELECT char_col FROM test_char);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_char WHERE char_col BETWEEN 'A' AND 'C';
CREATE TABLE complex_char_test (id INT, char_col CHARACTER(1), another_col INT);
INSERT INTO complex_char_test (id, char_col, another_col) VALUES (1, 'A', 10), (2, 'B', 20);
SELECT DISTINCT char_col FROM complex_char_test WHERE another_col > 15;
